perceptual_findings.female_1750: Complete target Hindi words became clearly recognizable for the first time.
perceptual_findings.female_2000_to_2250: Pronunciation and target-word recognition continued to improve.
perceptual_findings.female_2500_to_2750: Several samples produced smoother and clearer sentence fragments.
perceptual_findings.female_3000: Best overall checkpoint across most samples. Multiple target words and near-correct sentence fragments were audible, with occasional fumbling and mispronunciation.

important_observation: Perceptual quality was sample-dependent. Checkpoints 2500 or 2750 occasionally sounded smoother than 3000, despite checkpoint 3000 performing best overall.
conclusion: Female-only specialization produced a clear progressive improvement over the base and mixed-speaker models. Checkpoint 3000 achieved the strongest overall Hindi pronunciation and target alignment, but fully accurate sentence-level intelligibility was not achieved consistently.
